8.reason: [Using 'gets' is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11 and causes compilation errors with modern compilers due to security risks. Use 'fgets' instead.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Using 'gets' is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11 and causes compilation errors with modern compilers due to security risks. Use 'fgets' instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Using 'gets' is unsafe and has been removed from the C standard; it may cause compilation errors with modern compilers. Use 'fgets' instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Using 'gets' is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11 and causes compilation errors with modern compilers. Use 'fgets' instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [The function 'gets' is unsafe and has been removed from the C standard library since C11; using it may cause compilation errors or warnings depending on compiler settings. It should be replaced with 'fgets'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [Using 'gets' is unsafe and deprecated; it may cause buffer overflow. More critically, in many modern environments, 'gets' is removed from the standard library, leading to a compilation error.]

16.reason: [Incorrect condition: should also check that s[i+1] >= '0' (48) to ensure both ends are digits. Similarly, missing lower bound checks for letters in other conditions.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Incorrect condition: should also check that s[i+1] >= '0' (48) to ensure both ends are digits. Similarly, missing lower bound checks for letters in other conditions.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Condition checks only upper bound for s[i+1] and lower bound for s[i-1]; should also ensure s[i-1] <= '9' and s[i+1] >= '0' to confirm both are digits.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Condition checks only upper bound for s[i+1] and lower bound for s[i-1]; should also ensure s[i-1] <= '9' and s[i+1] >= '0' to confirm both are digits.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [This condition checks only the upper bound of s[i+1] for digits but does not verify that s[i+1] >= '0'; similarly, it doesn't ensure s[i-1] <= '9'. Thus, it may incorrectly process cases like '-0' or '9-:' as valid digit ranges.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [Incorrect condition: should also check that s[i+1] >= '0' (i.e., >=48) to ensure both ends are digits. As written, e.g., '-9' with s[i-1]='-' (ASCII 45) could pass if s[i+1] <= '9', which is wrong. Also, missing validation that s[i+1] is a digit.]

21.reason: [Incorrect condition: should also check that s[i+1] >= 'A' (65) to ensure both ends are uppercase letters.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Incorrect condition: should also check that s[i+1] >= 'A' (65) to ensure both ends are uppercase letters.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Condition checks only upper bound for s[i+1] and lower bound for s[i-1]; should also ensure s[i-1] <= 'Z' and s[i+1] >= 'A' to confirm both are uppercase letters.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Condition checks only upper bound for s[i+1] and lower bound for s[i-1]; should also ensure s[i-1] <= 'Z' and s[i+1] >= 'A' to confirm both are uppercase letters.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [This condition checks only the upper bound of s[i+1] for uppercase letters but does not verify that s[i+1] >= 'A'; similarly, it doesn't ensure s[i-1] <= 'Z'. Thus, it may incorrectly process mixed-type cases like 'Z-a' if a happens to be <= 'Z' in ASCII (which it isn't), but more critically, it fails to validate both ends are uppercase.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [Incorrect condition: should also check that s[i+1] >= 'A' (i.e., >=65). Without it, a case like 'Z-' followed by a character less than 'Z' but still <= 'Z' (e.g., '0') might incorrectly trigger expansion.]

26.reason: [Incorrect condition: should also check that s[i+1] >= 'a' (97) to ensure both ends are lowercase letters.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Incorrect condition: should also check that s[i+1] >= 'a' (97) to ensure both ends are lowercase letters.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Condition checks only upper bound for s[i+1] and lower bound for s[i-1]; should also ensure s[i-1] <= 'z' and s[i+1] >= 'a' to confirm both are lowercase letters.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Condition checks only upper bound for s[i+1] and lower bound for s[i-1]; should also ensure s[i-1] <= 'z' and s[i+1] >= 'a' to confirm both are lowercase letters.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [This condition checks only the upper bound of s[i+1] for lowercase letters but does not verify that s[i+1] >= 'a'; similarly, it doesn't ensure s[i-1] <= 'z'. Thus, it may incorrectly process invalid ranges involving non-lowercase characters.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [Incorrect condition: should also check that s[i+1] >= 'a' (i.e., >=97). Otherwise, non-lowercase characters after '-' could be misinterpreted as valid.]
